items.title: Product Manager
items.sector: Internet Marketplace Platforms and Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage
items.description: About PorterPorter is India's largest intra-city and inter-city logistics platform, connecting millions of customers with a trusted network of driver-partners. We are building technology-first products that redefine logistics, making it faster, smarter, and more reliable for businesses and consumers alike.About the RoleWe are looking for an experienced Product Manager to own a full product charter at Porter. You will be accountable for the charter's OKRs, from problem discovery and roadmap planning through delivery, iteration, and business impact.You are expected to operate with high independence, run multiple concurrent discovery initiatives, manage a 2–3 quarter roadmap, and actively drive cross-functional teams to deliver outcomes.Key ResponsibilitiesOwn the roadmap and OKRs for a full product charter (2–3 quarters ahead)Create detailed PRDs with problem statements, requirements, launch milestones, instrumentation, and impact sizingDesign and execute multiple concurrent problem and solution discovery initiatives; synthesize insights from diverse data sources and research methodsBuild systematic frameworks to validate and refine solution options across the charterDrive cross-functional execution with engineering, design, business, and data teams; proactively resolve delivery roadblocksEstablish a metrics-driven approach for monitoring delivery efficiency and product impactCoach junior PMs on discovery techniques, PRD quality, and delivery best practicesLead go-to-market planning and support operational readiness for product launchesWhat We Are Looking ForMust Have4–7 years of product management experience, with at least 2 years on B2C consumer-facing products at meaningful scaleProven ability to own a full product charter with measurable OKR accountabilityStrong product discovery skills: ability to synthesise insights from multiple research methods and data sources simultaneouslyExcellent data fluency: sharp metric definitions, impact sizing, data-informed prioritisation, and anomaly-to-action capabilityExperience with roadmap planning and backlog management (2–3 quarter horizon)Ability to influence without authority across engineering, design, and business teamsStructured, clear communication with stakeholders across levelsExperience with A/B experimentation and iterative product developmentGood to HaveExperience on platform, growth, engagement, or personalisationPrior mentoring of junior PMs or cross-functional team membersBackground at a product-led B2C company with scale (>1Mn MAU)
items.descriptionHtml:
About Porter

Porter is India's largest intra-city and inter-city logistics platform, connecting millions of customers with a trusted network of driver-partners. We are building technology-first products that redefine logistics, making it faster, smarter, and more reliable for businesses and consumers alike.


About the Role

We are looking for an experienced Product Manager to own a full product charter at Porter. You will be accountable for the charter's OKRs, from problem discovery and roadmap planning through delivery, iteration, and business impact.

You are expected to operate with high independence, run multiple concurrent discovery initiatives, manage a 2–3 quarter roadmap, and actively drive cross-functional teams to deliver outcomes.


Key Responsibilities

  • Own the roadmap and OKRs for a full product charter (2–3 quarters ahead)
  • Create detailed PRDs with problem statements, requirements, launch milestones, instrumentation, and impact sizing
  • Design and execute multiple concurrent problem and solution discovery initiatives; synthesize insights from diverse data sources and research methods
  • Build systematic frameworks to validate and refine solution options across the charter
  • Drive cross-functional execution with engineering, design, business, and data teams; proactively resolve delivery roadblocks
  • Establish a metrics-driven approach for monitoring delivery efficiency and product impact
  • Coach junior PMs on discovery techniques, PRD quality, and delivery best practices
  • Lead go-to-market planning and support operational readiness for product launches


What We Are Looking For

Must Have

  • 4–7 years of product management experience, with at least 2 years on B2C consumer-facing products at meaningful scale
  • Proven ability to own a full product charter with measurable OKR accountability
  • Strong product discovery skills: ability to synthesise insights from multiple research methods and data sources simultaneously
  • Excellent data fluency: sharp metric definitions, impact sizing, data-informed prioritisation, and anomaly-to-action capability
  • Experience with roadmap planning and backlog management (2–3 quarter horizon)
  • Ability to influence without authority across engineering, design, and business teams
  • Structured, clear communication with stakeholders across levels
  • Experience with A/B experimentation and iterative product development

Good to Have

  • Experience on platform, growth, engagement, or personalisation
  • Prior mentoring of junior PMs or cross-functional team members
  • Background at a product-led B2C company with scale (>1Mn MAU)

items.title: Product Manager - Supply Chain
items.sector: Food and Beverage Services, Internet Marketplace Platforms, and Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage
items.description: We are looking for a Product Manager – Supply Chain to drive product strategy and execution across Licious’ farm-to-fork ecosystem. items.descriptionHtml:
We are looking for a Product Manager – Supply Chain to drive product strategy and execution across Licious’ farm-to-fork ecosystem. This role will work across sourcing, procurement, demand planning, inventory, warehousing, and logistics systems to build scalable, data-driven solutions.

You will own end-to-end supply chain product initiatives that improve service levels, reduce wastage, and optimize fulfillment costs, while enabling scalable growth. Working closely with business, engineering, and operations teams, you will solve complex supply chain challenges in a fast-paced D2C environment.


Key Responsibilities

  • Product Strategy & OwnershipDefine and drive product vision, roadmap, and execution across supply chain domains such as procurement, demand planning, inventory management, warehouse management, and last-mile logistics
  • Own end-to-end product lifecycle from discovery to delivery and iteration
  • Problem Solving & ExecutionIdentify inefficiencies and bottlenecks in supply chain processes and translate them into scalable tech solutions
  • Break down ambiguous problems into structured product requirements and prioritize based on impact
  • Stakeholder ManagementCollaborate with cross-functional teams including Operations, Category, Finance, and Engineering
  • Act as a bridge between business and technology teams to ensure alignment and timely delivery
  • Data-Driven Decision MakingLeverage data to improve forecasting accuracy, inventory planning, and operational efficiency
  • Define, track, and optimize key supply chain metrics (fill rate, wastage, SLA adherence, inventory turns, etc.)
  • Execution & DeliveryWork closely with engineering teams in an agile setup to deliver high-quality, scalable products
  • Drive sprint planning, backlog prioritization, and release management
  • Business & Customer ImpactEnsure product solutions improve both customer experience (availability, freshness, delivery timelines) and operational cost efficiency


Required Qualifications

  • 2–5 years of experience in Product Management, preferably in supply chain, logistics, e-commerce, or D2C startups
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a structured approach
  • Hands-on experience working with data (SQL, Excel, dashboards, or analytics tools)
  • Ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders without formal authority
  • Good understanding of product development lifecycle and agile methodologies


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in supply chain domains such as inventory management, demand forecasting, warehouse systems, or logistics tech
  • Exposure to high-growth startup environments
  • Basic understanding of system design, APIs, and integrations
  • MBA or equivalent experience is a plus


What Success Looks Like

  • Improved supply chain efficiency through reduced wastage and better fill rates
  • Scalable and reliable systems supporting procurement, inventory, and logistics operations
  • Enhanced visibility and faster decision-making through data-driven products

items.title: Senior Product Manager - Supply Chain
items.sector: Food and Beverage Services and Internet Marketplace Platforms
items.description: As a Senior Product Manager – Supply Chain Management, you will own and evolve the technology platforms and workflows that power Licious’ end-to-end supply chain. You will work closely with Supply Chain, Operations, Data, Engineering, and Finance teams to build products that improve freshness, reduce waste, and enable scalable growth.This role requires strong items.descriptionHtml:
As a Senior Product Manager – Supply Chain Management, you will own and evolve the technology platforms and workflows that power Licious’ end-to-end supply chain. You will work closely with Supply Chain, Operations, Data, Engineering, and Finance teams to build products that improve freshness, reduce waste, and enable scalable growth.

This role requires strong product judgment, comfort with operational complexity, and the ability to balance speed, reliability, and long-term platform thinking.



What makes the role meaty...


Product Strategy & Ownership

  • Own the product vision and roadmap for supply chain systems across demand planning, procurement, production planning, warehousing, inventory, and logistics
  • Translate business goals (freshness, fill rate, cost, SLA adherence) into clear product outcomes and success metrics
  • Identify automation and digitization opportunities across supply chain workflows

Execution & Delivery

  • Lead discovery, prioritization, and delivery of features across internal platforms
  • Write high-quality PRDs, define user journeys, and ensure on-time execution with engineering and design teams
  • Drive experimentation and continuous improvement using data and operational feedback

Demand, Inventory & Planning

  • Build products that improve forecast accuracy, replenishment, and inventory turns
  • Partner with Data Science teams on forecasting models and decision-support tools
  • Enable real-time visibility into inventory, wastage, and capacity across nodes

Operations & Reliability

  • Design systems that ensure traceability, compliance, and quality control
  • Improve exception management, alerts, and operational dashboards
  • Balance system reliability with rapid iteration in a 24x7 operational environment

Stakeholder Management

  • Act as the product SPOC for Supply Chain and Operations leadership
  • Collaborate with Finance, Category, and Retail teams on cost, margins, and availability
  • Influence roadmap trade-offs by balancing operational needs and tech scalability


We hope that you have..


Experience

  • 6–10 years of product management experience, preferably in Supply Chain, Logistics, Manufacturing, Retail Ops, or Food/FMCG
  • Hands-on experience building internal platforms or operational products
  • Proven track record of shipping complex products in high-scale environments

Skills & Capabilities

  • Strong problem-solving and structured thinking
  • Data-driven mindset; comfort working with metrics, dashboards, and models
  • Ability to work with ambiguity and evolving operational constraints
  • Excellent stakeholder communication and influence skills

Good to Have

  • Experience in fresh food, cold chain, or perishable supply chains
  • Exposure to demand forecasting, inventory optimization, or WMS/TMS systems
  • Experience working with data science and optimization problems

items.title: Product Manager
items.description: Sumo Logic is seeking a Product Manager to define the future of our Security Operations platform. As a member of our product team, you will drive the vision and execution of Threat Detection and Response and define the intersection of AI and SecOps. Working across engineering, customer success, sales, and marketing, you will transform complex challenges into intuitive, powerful solutions that enable security teams to: detect threats faster, investigate with confidence, and respond decisively. This is an opportunity to shape products that directly impact how enterprises defend against evolving cyber threats while delivering measurable business value to customers.What You Will Be DoingDefining the vision, strategy, and roadmap for an exceptional user experience and innovative Threat Detection and Response features that drive customer engagement and satisfaction across our platform.Collaborating with customers and stakeholders to gather and analyze user feedback, use cases, and requirements to ensure we meet their needs while enhancing the overall platform user experience.Maintaining a detailed product roadmap supported by data analysis and competitive positioning to prioritize features and enhancements effectively.Working with cross-functional teams to identify and gather platform requirements that enhance user experience and dashboard functionalities.Representing the voice of the customer to product engineering teams and executives, ensuring user needs are at the forefront of product development.Partnering with engineering teams to ensure our dashboarding solutions are scalable, reliable, and performant, making prioritization decisions as necessary.Contributing to go-to-market strategies, collaborating with product marketing and sales to effectively communicate the value of our user experience and dashboarding capabilities.Developing sales enablement materials, including whitepapers, blogs, presentations, and product demos to showcase our dashboarding features.What You Bring3+ years of experience in product management, preferably with a focus on cybersecurity, and a proven track record of success in executionStrong understanding of security operations technologies (SIEM, SOAR, XDR, Threat Intelligence)Experience in working with B2B SaaS products and the ability to navigate complex decision-making environments.Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with a proven track record of effectively engaging with customers and cross-functional teams.A data-driven mindset, with the ability to use analytics to inform product decisions and prioritize development efforts.Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; an MBA is a plus. Why Join Sumo Logic?You will be working on one of the largest scale data problems in the industry. Your work directly impacts the company’s bottom line and determines our ability to innovate by freeing up capital for new product investments.About UsSumo Logic, Inc. helps make the digital world secure, fast, and reliable by unifying critical security and operational data through its Intelligent Operations Platform. Built to address the increasing complexity of modern cybersecurity and cloud operations challenges, we empower digital teams to move from reaction to readiness—combining agentic AI-powered SIEM and log analytics into a single platform to detect, investigate, and resolve modern challenges. Customers around the world rely on Sumo Logic for trusted insights to protect against security threats, ensure reliability, and gain powerful insights into their digital environments. For more information, visit www.sumologic.com.Sumo Logic Privacy Policy. Employees will be responsible for complying with applicable federal privacy laws and regulations, as well as organizational policies related to data protection.
items.descriptionHtml:
Sumo Logic is seeking a Product Manager to define the future of our Security Operations platform. As a member of our product team, you will drive the vision and execution of Threat Detection and Response and define the intersection of AI and SecOps. Working across engineering, customer success, sales, and marketing, you will transform complex challenges into intuitive, powerful solutions that enable security teams to: detect threats faster, investigate with confidence, and respond decisively. This is an opportunity to shape products that directly impact how enterprises defend against evolving cyber threats while delivering measurable business value to customers.

What You Will Be Doing

  • Defining the vision, strategy, and roadmap for an exceptional user experience and innovative Threat Detection and Response features that drive customer engagement and satisfaction across our platform.
  • Collaborating with customers and stakeholders to gather and analyze user feedback, use cases, and requirements to ensure we meet their needs while enhancing the overall platform user experience.
  • Maintaining a detailed product roadmap supported by data analysis and competitive positioning to prioritize features and enhancements effectively.
  • Working with cross-functional teams to identify and gather platform requirements that enhance user experience and dashboard functionalities.
  • Representing the voice of the customer to product engineering teams and executives, ensuring user needs are at the forefront of product development.
  • Partnering with engineering teams to ensure our dashboarding solutions are scalable, reliable, and performant, making prioritization decisions as necessary.
  • Contributing to go-to-market strategies, collaborating with product marketing and sales to effectively communicate the value of our user experience and dashboarding capabilities.
  • Developing sales enablement materials, including whitepapers, blogs, presentations, and product demos to showcase our dashboarding features.

What You Bring

  • 3+ years of experience in product management, preferably with a focus on cybersecurity, and a proven track record of success in execution
  • Strong understanding of security operations technologies (SIEM, SOAR, XDR, Threat Intelligence)
  • Experience in working with B2B SaaS products and the ability to navigate complex decision-making environments.
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with a proven track record of effectively engaging with customers and cross-functional teams.
  • A data-driven mindset, with the ability to use analytics to inform product decisions and prioritize development efforts.
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field; an MBA is a plus.

Why Join Sumo Logic?

You will be working on one of the largest scale data problems in the industry. Your work directly impacts the company’s bottom line and determines our ability to innovate by freeing up capital for new product investments.

About Us

Sumo Logic, Inc. helps make the digital world secure, fast, and reliable by unifying critical security and operational data through its Intelligent Operations Platform. Built to address the increasing complexity of modern cybersecurity and cloud operations challenges, we empower digital teams to move from reaction to readiness—combining agentic AI-powered SIEM and log analytics into a single platform to detect, investigate, and resolve modern challenges. Customers around the world rely on Sumo Logic for trusted insights to protect against security threats, ensure reliability, and gain powerful insights into their digital environments. For more information, visit www.sumologic.com.

Sumo Logic Privacy Policy. Employees will be responsible for complying with applicable federal privacy laws and regulations, as well as organizational policies related to data protection.

items.title: Product Manager
items.description: Who we areDigiCert is a global leader in intelligent trust. We protect the digital world by ensuring the security, privacy, and authenticity of every interaction. Our AI-powered DigiCert ONE platform unifies PKI, DNS, and certificate lifecycle management, to secure infrastructure, software, devices, messages, AI content and agents. Learn why more than 100,000 organizations, including 90% of the Fortune 500, choose DigiCert to stop today’s threats and prepare for a quantum-safe future at www.digicert.com  Job summaryDigiCert is redefining the Domain Name System as an AI-native trust layer — enabling secure, intelligent, and autonomous resolution for the next era of the Internet.As a Product Manager, you will lead innovation at the intersection of AI, DNS, and digital trust. You will collaborate with engineering, and GTM teams to build cloud-native DNS services that use inference and automation to detect anomalies, optimize performance, and strengthen security. You will also leverage AI tools to move fast — turning ideas and insights into a clear, measurable roadmap. What you will doDefine and own the AI-driven DNS roadmap, aligned with DigiCert’s trust and cloud strategy.Translate complex AI/ML capabilities into customer-focused, market-ready features.Partner with engineering and MLOps teams to deliver secure, scalable, cloud-native services.Use AI-assisted tools to analyze feedback, synthesize insights, and iterate quickly.Define and track success metrics — adoption, latency, reliability, trust-signal accuracy.Drive GTM execution with marketing and sales — launches, messaging, pricing, enablement.Engage customers, partners, and standards groups to validate use cases and guide innovation.Foster a data-driven product culture using telemetry, dashboards, and A/B experimentation.Ship production-ready features in days, not weeks, and iterate continuously. What you will have4+ years of product-management experience in cloud, networking, or infrastructure SaaS.Experience working closely with engineering teams on AWS, Azure or GCP.Understanding of DNS, networking, or distributed-systems fundamentals.Familiarity with AI/ML systems — especially inference pipelines or MLOps workflows (desired).Proven ability to deliver 0→1 products and iterate rapidly in agile environments.Strong analytical and storytelling skills; fluent in data-driven decision-making.Leverage AI-assisted tools (e.g., Figma Make, Cursor, vibe coding, Copilot) to:Rapidly prototype workflows and UI conceptsValidate ideas early with engineers and customersIterate on requirements before buildExperience supporting or leading GTM activities (launches, collateral, pricing, adoption tracking).Self-starter with an ownership mindset and a passion for 0→1 building in fast-moving environments. Nice to haveStartup speed, enterprise scale.Builders, not operators.Security, trust, and automation by default.AI-accelerated, open-source driven, cloud-native by design. BenefitsGenerous time off policiesTop shelf benefitsEducation, wellness and lifestyle support
items.descriptionHtml:
Who we are

DigiCert is a global leader in intelligent trust. We protect the digital world by ensuring the security, privacy, and authenticity of every interaction. Our AI-powered DigiCert ONE platform unifies PKI, DNS, and certificate lifecycle management, to secure infrastructure, software, devices, messages, AI content and agents. Learn why more than 100,000 organizations, including 90% of the Fortune 500, choose DigiCert to stop today’s threats and prepare for a quantum-safe future at www.digicert.com

 

Job summary

DigiCert is redefining the Domain Name System as an AI-native trust layer — enabling secure, intelligent, and autonomous resolution for the next era of the Internet.

As a Product Manager, you will lead innovation at the intersection of AI, DNS, and digital trust. You will collaborate with engineering, and GTM teams to build cloud-native DNS services that use inference and automation to detect anomalies, optimize performance, and strengthen security. You will also leverage AI tools to move fast — turning ideas and insights into a clear, measurable roadmap.

 

What you will do

  • Define and own the AI-driven DNS roadmap, aligned with DigiCert’s trust and cloud strategy.
  • Translate complex AI/ML capabilities into customer-focused, market-ready features.
  • Partner with engineering and MLOps teams to deliver secure, scalable, cloud-native services.
  • Use AI-assisted tools to analyze feedback, synthesize insights, and iterate quickly.
  • Define and track success metrics — adoption, latency, reliability, trust-signal accuracy.
  • Drive GTM execution with marketing and sales — launches, messaging, pricing, enablement.
  • Engage customers, partners, and standards groups to validate use cases and guide innovation.
  • Foster a data-driven product culture using telemetry, dashboards, and A/B experimentation.
  • Ship production-ready features in days, not weeks, and iterate continuously.

 

What you will have

  • 4+ years of product-management experience in cloud, networking, or infrastructure SaaS.
  • Experience working closely with engineering teams on AWS, Azure or GCP.
  • Understanding of DNS, networking, or distributed-systems fundamentals.
  • Familiarity with AI/ML systems — especially inference pipelines or MLOps workflows (desired).
  • Proven ability to deliver 0→1 products and iterate rapidly in agile environments.
  • Strong analytical and storytelling skills; fluent in data-driven decision-making.
  • Leverage AI-assisted tools (e.g., Figma Make, Cursor, vibe coding, Copilot) to:
    • Rapidly prototype workflows and UI concepts
    • Validate ideas early with engineers and customers
    • Iterate on requirements before build
  • Experience supporting or leading GTM activities (launches, collateral, pricing, adoption tracking).
  • Self-starter with an ownership mindset and a passion for 0→1 building in fast-moving environments.

 

Nice to have

  • Startup speed, enterprise scale.
  • Builders, not operators.
  • Security, trust, and automation by default.
  • AI-accelerated, open-source driven, cloud-native by design.

 

Benefits

  • Generous time off policies
  • Top shelf benefits
  • Education, wellness and lifestyle support

items.title: Product Manager
items.description: About CompanyMasai School is a fast-growing outcome-driven EdTech platform focused on building careers through industry-relevant learning programs. We are building scalable products that impact thousands of learners and organizations across India.We are looking for a highly driven Product Manager with strong startup experience who can take ownership of product strategy, execution, and growth for our SaaS products.Role: Product ManagerExperience: 5- 8 YearsLocation: BangaloreKey ResponsibilitiesOwn the end-to-end product lifecycle from ideation to launch and optimizationWork closely with founders, business teams, engineering, design, and operations teamsGather customer insights, analyze product gaps, and identify new opportunitiesDefine product roadmap, priorities, and execution timelinesTranslate business requirements into clear product requirements and user storiesDrive product adoption, engagement, retention, and overall user experienceMonitor product metrics, user behavior, and business impact to make data-driven decisionsConduct market research and competitor analysis for SaaS productsCoordinate with tech and design teams to ensure timely product deliveryContinuously improve product workflows, automation, and scalabilityRequirements5 - 8 years of experience in Product ManagementPrior experience working in a startup environment is mandatoryStrong understanding of SaaS products and product lifecycle managementExperience working with cross-functional teams including tech, design, and business stakeholdersStrong analytical and problem-solving skillsAbility to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environmentExcellent communication and stakeholder management skillsHands-on experience with tools like Jira, Notion, Mixpanel, GA, or similar product tools is preferredUnderstanding of user journeys, product funnels, and growth metricsPreferred QualificationsExperience in EdTech, SaaS, or consumer tech productsExposure to product-led growth strategiesStrong ownership mindset with execution-focused approachWhy Join Masai?Opportunity to build high-impact products at scaleFast-paced startup culture with strong ownershipWork directly with leadership and cross-functional teamsCareer growth and learning opportunities in a rapidly scaling companySkills: startup experience,design,product lifecycle management,product manager,user experience,saas products,saas,product management,edtech
items.descriptionHtml:
About Company

Masai School is a fast-growing outcome-driven EdTech platform focused on building careers through industry-relevant learning programs. We are building scalable products that impact thousands of learners and organizations across India.

We are looking for a highly driven Product Manager with strong startup experience who can take ownership of product strategy, execution, and growth for our SaaS products.

Role: Product Manager

Experience: 5- 8 Years

Location: Bangalore

Key Responsibilities

  • Own the end-to-end product lifecycle from ideation to launch and optimization
  • Work closely with founders, business teams, engineering, design, and operations teams
  • Gather customer insights, analyze product gaps, and identify new opportunities
  • Define product roadmap, priorities, and execution timelines
  • Translate business requirements into clear product requirements and user stories
  • Drive product adoption, engagement, retention, and overall user experience
  • Monitor product metrics, user behavior, and business impact to make data-driven decisions
  • Conduct market research and competitor analysis for SaaS products
  • Coordinate with tech and design teams to ensure timely product delivery
  • Continuously improve product workflows, automation, and scalability

Requirements

  • 5 - 8 years of experience in Product Management
  • Prior experience working in a startup environment is mandatory
  • Strong understanding of SaaS products and product lifecycle management
  • Experience working with cross-functional teams including tech, design, and business stakeholders
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
  • Hands-on experience with tools like Jira, Notion, Mixpanel, GA, or similar product tools is preferred
  • Understanding of user journeys, product funnels, and growth metrics

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in EdTech, SaaS, or consumer tech products
  • Exposure to product-led growth strategies
  • Strong ownership mindset with execution-focused approach

Why Join Masai?

  • Opportunity to build high-impact products at scale
  • Fast-paced startup culture with strong ownership
  • Work directly with leadership and cross-functional teams
  • Career growth and learning opportunities in a rapidly scaling company

Skills: startup experience,design,product lifecycle management,product manager,user experience,saas products,saas,product management,edtech

items.title: Product Owner Manager
items.sector: Software Development, Computer and Network Security, and Information Technology & Services
items.description: Revenera helps product executives build better products, accelerate time to value and monetize what matters. Revenera’s leading solutions help software and technology companies drive top line revenue with modern software monetization, understand usage and compliance with software usage analytics, empower the use of open source with software composition analysis and deliver an excellent user experience—for embedded, on premises, cloud, and SaaS products.Revenera helps product executives build better products, accelerate time to value and monetize what matters. Revenera’s leading solutions help software and technology companies drive top line revenue with modern software monetization, understand usage and compliance with software usage analytics, and deliver an excellent user experience—for embedded, on-premises, cloud, and SaaS products.Our software monetization platform supports licensing, entitlement management, and usage analytics and is used by leading software and technology companies to enable their business models, prevent revenue leakage, and gain insight into their software businesses. Our installation software enables safe, reliable delivery experiences, allowing development teams to focus on what matters most.We are looking for a highly motivated, experienced Product Owner and People Manager in India to play a key role in the agile development of Software Monetization solutions.You will manage a team of Product Owners responsible for driving feature development, product enhancements, and roadmap execution, ensuring the voice of the customer and the business is fully represented within the agile process. The team works closely with architects, product managers, marketing, and engineering to ensure business, quality, and functional objectives are met with every release.The Product Owner team ensures that product updates align with expectations, meet delivery commitments, and provide clear visibility into progress, impact, and risks. As the team manager, you are accountable for the team’s professional development, motivation, and consistent application of agile and product best practices.You will work closely with Strategic Product Managers to understand the defined roadmap and partner with Engineering teams to successfully deliver product increments, modelling operational excellence.Job DescriptionWorking across the Software Monetization product lines, help deliver value through FlexNet Operations, FlexNet Licensing, and compliance solutions.ResponsibilitiesLead and support the Product Owner team through regular one‑to‑one and team meetings, building confidence, ownership, and delivery maturityApply and promote agile principles and effective product ownership practices within Product Management and across ReveneraTrack individual and team progress against goals, supporting both delivery outcomes and professional growthOwn the quality of epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria, while actively Regarding disability, we encourage candidates requiring accommodations to please let us know by emailing careers@flexera.com.
items.descriptionHtml:
Revenera helps product executives build better products, accelerate time to value and monetize what matters. Revenera’s leading solutions help software and technology companies drive top line revenue with modern software monetization, understand usage and compliance with software usage analytics, empower the use of open source with software composition analysis and deliver an excellent user experience—for embedded, on premises, cloud, and SaaS products.

Revenera helps product executives build better products, accelerate time to value and monetize what matters. Revenera’s leading solutions help software and technology companies drive top line revenue with modern software monetization, understand usage and compliance with software usage analytics, and deliver an excellent user experience—for embedded, on-premises, cloud, and SaaS products.

Our software monetization platform supports licensing, entitlement management, and usage analytics and is used by leading software and technology companies to enable their business models, prevent revenue leakage, and gain insight into their software businesses. Our installation software enables safe, reliable delivery experiences, allowing development teams to focus on what matters most.

We are looking for a highly motivated, experienced Product Owner and People Manager in India to play a key role in the agile development of Software Monetization solutions.

You will manage a team of Product Owners responsible for driving feature development, product enhancements, and roadmap execution, ensuring the voice of the customer and the business is fully represented within the agile process. The team works closely with architects, product managers, marketing, and engineering to ensure business, quality, and functional objectives are met with every release.

The Product Owner team ensures that product updates align with expectations, meet delivery commitments, and provide clear visibility into progress, impact, and risks. As the team manager, you are accountable for the team’s professional development, motivation, and consistent application of agile and product best practices.

You will work closely with Strategic Product Managers to understand the defined roadmap and partner with Engineering teams to successfully deliver product increments, modelling operational excellence.

Job Description

Working across the Software Monetization product lines, help deliver value through FlexNet Operations, FlexNet Licensing, and compliance solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Lead and support the Product Owner team through regular one‑to‑one and team meetings, building confidence, ownership, and delivery maturity
  • Apply and promote agile principles and effective product ownership practices within Product Management and across Revenera
  • Track individual and team progress against goals, supporting both delivery outcomes and professional growth
  • Own the quality of epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria, while actively coaching Product Owners on backlog management and stakeholder collaboration
  • Guide roadmap elaboration to achieve clarity of scope, effort, and value, enabling informed prioritisation and trade‑off decisions
  • Identify and remove delivery impediments, escalating effectively when required
  • Support Product Management with customer insight, research, and structured feedback from internal and external stakeholders
  • Act as a trusted product representative to engineering, leadership, and support teams
  • Participate in customer interactions, particularly for validating new functionality and understanding enhancement or defect trends

Required Skills And Knowledge

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical product experience
  • Strong experience as a Product Owner or Senior Product Owner, with early people‑management or mentoring responsibility
  • Demonstrated ability to lead through influence, structure, and clarity, even when formal authority is still developing
  • Experience delivering complex SaaS or enterprise software products
  • Working knowledge of multi‑platform, on‑premises software development, including embedded or SDK‑based systems
  • Solid understanding of B2B software operating in air‑gapped, hybrid, and online environments
  • Practical experience with agile software development methodologies
  • Strong organisational, prioritisation, and decision‑making skills
  • Clear written and verbal English communication skills for working with global stakeholders
  • Willingness to collaborate across time zones, including occasional evening meetings
  • A growth mindset with the ambition to progress toward broader people‑leadership responsibility

Revenera is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered for open roles regardless of age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local/national laws, policies and/or regulations. Regarding disability, we encourage candidates requiring accommodations to please let us know by emailing careers@flexera.com.

items.title: Senior Product Manager - RPay
items.description: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TVRoku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.  About the Team & Role The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business. This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. What You’ll Be Doing Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction, payment routing, and fraud prevention.Champion Experimentation:  Design and oversee A/B and multivariate tests to validate hypotheses, measure incremental lift, and drive continuous improvement.Hands-On Execution: Roll up your sleeves to write PRDs, define acceptance criteria, review model performance dashboards, and troubleshoot issues with engineering.Stakeholder Management: Communicate roadmap progress, risks, and learnings to executives and cross-functional partners.Post-Launch Optimization: Monitor KPIs, identify opportunities for improvement, and prioritize enhancements to maximize impact.Ensure Compliance & Security: Work with legal and compliance teams to meet PCI-DSS, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements for payment data.  We’re Excited If You Have 8+ years in product management, ideally with experience in payments, fintech, subscription businesses, or ML-driven platforms. Proven track record of shipping complex products in partnership with engineering, data science, and operations teams. Strong understanding of payment processing flows, gateways, acquirer optimization, and retry logic. Experience defining and measuring KPIs, running large-scale A/B tests, and making data-driven decisions. Ability to articulate complex ML concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Comfort working in fast-paced, highly iterative environments. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field; MBA a plus.    Our Hybrid Work ApproachRoku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy. BenefitsRoku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter. AccommodationsRoku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com. The Roku CultureRoku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.
items.descriptionHtml:
Teamwork makes the stream work.

 

Roku is changing how the world watches TV

Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.

From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.

 

About the Team & Role 

The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.

Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business.
This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical.
The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

  • Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.
  • Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. 
  • Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction, payment routing, and fraud prevention.
  • Champion Experimentation:  Design and oversee A/B and multivariate tests to validate hypotheses, measure incremental lift, and drive continuous improvement.
  • Hands-On Execution: Roll up your sleeves to write PRDs, define acceptance criteria, review model performance dashboards, and troubleshoot issues with engineering.
  • Stakeholder Management: Communicate roadmap progress, risks, and learnings to executives and cross-functional partners.
  • Post-Launch Optimization: Monitor KPIs, identify opportunities for improvement, and prioritize enhancements to maximize impact.
  • Ensure Compliance & Security: Work with legal and compliance teams to meet PCI-DSS, GDPR, and other regulatory requirements for payment data. 

 

We’re Excited If You Have 

  • 8+ years in product management, ideally with experience in payments, fintech, subscription businesses, or ML-driven platforms. 
  • Proven track record of shipping complex products in partnership with engineering, data science, and operations teams. 
  • Strong understanding of payment processing flows, gateways, acquirer optimization, and retry logic. 
  • Experience defining and measuring KPIs, running large-scale A/B tests, and making data-driven decisions. 
  • Ability to articulate complex ML concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. 
  • Comfort working in fast-paced, highly iterative environments. 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, or related field; MBA a plus. 
 

 

Our Hybrid Work Approach

Roku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy.

 

Benefits

Roku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Employees are supported in taking time off, in accordance with local leave policies and other personal needs to support their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter.

 

Accommodations

Roku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com.

 

The Roku Culture

Roku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. 

We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. 

To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet.

By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.

items.title: Product Manager
items.description: About the Role We're looking for an AI Product Manager who builds, not just specs. This is not a traditional PM role. You won't write requirements and wait for designers or developers to pick them up. You'll use Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI tools to prototype working solutions yourself. You'll ship fast, learn from users, and iterate. What You'll Do Product Discovery & Strategy • Own the roadmap for core organic growth platform features • Run continuous discovery: user interviews, data analysis, competitor research • Identify gaps and opportunities before they're obvious • Make prioritization calls based on user impact and business value Build & Ship • Prototype working solutions using AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Replit) • Work with engineers to refine and ship production-ready features • Move from idea to testable prototype in hours, not weeks • Maintain a high-quality bar while moving fast.Research & Competitive Intelligence • Track competitors obsessively: new features, positioning, pricing moves • Stay ahead of AI search platform changes (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI) • Synthesize trends into actionable product insights • Share intelligence across the team regularly Data & Optimization • Live in product analytics tools daily (Amplitude, Mix panel, or similar) • Define and track key KPIs for your features • Run experiments and measure impact • Use data to kill features that aren't workingThe profile we are looking For Builder PM • You prototype solutions, not just document requirements • You use AI tools to move at 10x speed • You have enough engineering background to be dangerous • Obsessed with competitors and market shifts • Balances speed with quality • Takes ownership of outcomes, not just outputs Must-Haves • 3+ years in product at a fast-moving startup (AI company preferred) • Hands-on experience with AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, or similar) • Some software engineering background (you can read and write code) • Strong UX instincts: you think like a user • Data-driven: you use analytics tools daily, not weekly • High ownership: you see problems and fix them without being asked • Taste: you know what good looks like AI Fluency (Non-Negotiable) • Power user of Claude Code, Cursor, or similar for daily work • Already using AI coding tools to prototype and build • Comfortable learning new AI tools in days, not months • Treats AI as how you work, not an add-on
items.descriptionHtml:
About the Role

We're looking for an AI Product Manager who builds, not just specs. This is not a traditional PM role. You won't write requirements and wait for designers or developers to pick them up.

You'll use Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI tools to prototype working solutions yourself. You'll ship fast, learn from users, and iterate.


What You'll Do

Product Discovery & Strategy

• Own the roadmap for core organic growth platform features

• Run continuous discovery: user interviews, data analysis, competitor research

• Identify gaps and opportunities before they're obvious

• Make prioritization calls based on user impact and business value


Build & Ship

• Prototype working solutions using AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Replit)

• Work with engineers to refine and ship production-ready features

• Move from idea to testable prototype in hours, not weeks

• Maintain a high-quality bar while moving fast.


Research & Competitive Intelligence

• Track competitors obsessively: new features, positioning, pricing moves

• Stay ahead of AI search platform changes (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI)

• Synthesize trends into actionable product insights

• Share intelligence across the team regularly


Data & Optimization

• Live in product analytics tools daily (Amplitude, Mix panel, or similar)

• Define and track key KPIs for your features

• Run experiments and measure impact

• Use data to kill features that aren't working


The profile we are looking For


Builder PM

• You prototype solutions, not just document requirements

• You use AI tools to move at 10x speed

• You have enough engineering background to be dangerous

• Obsessed with competitors and market shifts

• Balances speed with quality

• Takes ownership of outcomes, not just outputs


Must-Haves

• 3+ years in product at a fast-moving startup (AI company preferred)

• Hands-on experience with AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, or similar)

• Some software engineering background (you can read and write code)

• Strong UX instincts: you think like a user

• Data-driven: you use analytics tools daily, not weekly

• High ownership: you see problems and fix them without being asked

• Taste: you know what good looks like


AI Fluency (Non-Negotiable)

• Power user of Claude Code, Cursor, or similar for daily work

• Already using AI coding tools to prototype and build

• Comfortable learning new AI tools in days, not months

• Treats AI as how you work, not an add-on

items.title: Product Manager
items.description: Product ManagerLocation: BangaloreAbout the TeamNavi’s Product Team builds solutions that are simple, scalable, and centered on real user needs. The team strongly collaborates across functions-engineering, design, data, and business-to turn insight into impact.About the RoleAs a Product Manager at Navi, you will champion the voice of the customer and drive the development of innovative financial solutions, aiming to solve finance for a billion people. You will be responsible for the end-to-end management of your product area the critical backend systems that power key financial decisions from conception through launch and beyond. Your work will directly influence the speed, accuracy, and fairness of financial products for millions of Indians.This role requires a proactive approach to understanding customer and system requirements through detailed market research, user testing, and deep data analysis. You will independently spearhead initiatives, define and analyse critical metrics to gauge product success, and lead a cross-functional team including engineering, design, data science, and more, to achieve outstanding product outcomes. This role is ideal for someone with a strong foundation in product thinking and a passion for delivering impactful solutions with increasing independence.Key Responsibilities- Develop a strong understanding of our customers and internal platform users, leveraging this insight to craft differentiated user experiences.- Independently define the roadmap and strategy for your product area, ensuring alignment with broader business objectives.- Formulate product strategy and recommendations primarily through hands-on data investigation and experimentation, prioritising quantitative insights to drive your roadmap.- Drive the development and continuous improvement of products based on thorough market research and user feedback.- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality products with minimal oversight.- Define, track, and immerse yourself in performance metrics, analysing the effectiveness of core decisioning models to uncover deep insights that guide all future product iteration.Must Haves- 4+ years of relevant product management experience, preferably in a fast-paced, digital-first environment.- Demonstrated empathy towards the needs, concerns, and experiences of end-users.- Strong foundation in technology and first-principles product thinking.- Advanced problem-solving skills with a structured and analytical approach.- Proven ability to make data-driven decisions through direct, hands-on quantitative analysis that leads to successful business outcomes.- A fundamental understanding of data science concepts and how they apply to product development is highly desirable.- Ownership mindset with a high drive for achieving excellence and winning in the market.- Excellent communication skills, capable of effectively articulating complex ideas to diverse audiences.- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business Administration, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.About NaviWe are on a mission to make finance simple, accessible and affordable for a billion Indians. Guided by a strong customer-first approach, we are building tech-first solutions that work at scale. Our offerings include a range of financial products and services across Loans, Insurance, Mutual Funds, Digital Gold and UPI.Founded by Sachin Bansal & Ankit Agarwal in 2018, we are one of India’s fastest-growing financial services organisations. But we’re just getting started!Our CultureAt Navi, we’re a place where ambition meets opportunity and ideas turn into impact quickly. We empower people with high ownership from the start, encouraging them to solve meaningful problems and build with excellence. Teams here work in an environment that values speed, collaboration and craftsmanship, while celebrating learning, growth and shared wins along the way. Whether you’re shaping the future of fintech products or driving innovation behind the scenes, life at Navi means being part of a high-energy, talent-driven workplace where your contributions truly make a difference.We’re guided by our own operating system - The Navi OS - a set of principles that shape how we work and win together. You can explore them at navi.com/our-values to see what drives us every day.If this feels like you, Navi is the place to grow, thrive and make a real impact.
items.descriptionHtml:
Product Manager


Location: Bangalore


About the Team

Navi’s Product Team builds solutions that are simple, scalable, and centered on real user needs. The team strongly collaborates across functions-engineering, design, data, and business-to turn insight into impact.


About the Role

As a Product Manager at Navi, you will champion the voice of the customer and drive the development of innovative financial solutions, aiming to solve finance for a billion people. You will be responsible for the end-to-end management of your product area the critical backend systems that power key financial decisions from conception through launch and beyond. Your work will directly influence the speed, accuracy, and fairness of financial products for millions of Indians.

This role requires a proactive approach to understanding customer and system requirements through detailed market research, user testing, and deep data analysis. You will independently spearhead initiatives, define and analyse critical metrics to gauge product success, and lead a cross-functional team including engineering, design, data science, and more, to achieve outstanding product outcomes. This role is ideal for someone with a strong foundation in product thinking and a passion for delivering impactful solutions with increasing independence.


Key Responsibilities

- Develop a strong understanding of our customers and internal platform users, leveraging this insight to craft differentiated user experiences.

- Independently define the roadmap and strategy for your product area, ensuring alignment with broader business objectives.

- Formulate product strategy and recommendations primarily through hands-on data investigation and experimentation, prioritising quantitative insights to drive your roadmap.

- Drive the development and continuous improvement of products based on thorough market research and user feedback.

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality products with minimal oversight.

- Define, track, and immerse yourself in performance metrics, analysing the effectiveness of core decisioning models to uncover deep insights that guide all future product iteration.


Must Haves

- 4+ years of relevant product management experience, preferably in a fast-paced, digital-first environment.

- Demonstrated empathy towards the needs, concerns, and experiences of end-users.

- Strong foundation in technology and first-principles product thinking.

- Advanced problem-solving skills with a structured and analytical approach.

- Proven ability to make data-driven decisions through direct, hands-on quantitative analysis that leads to successful business outcomes.

- A fundamental understanding of data science concepts and how they apply to product development is highly desirable.

- Ownership mindset with a high drive for achieving excellence and winning in the market.

- Excellent communication skills, capable of effectively articulating complex ideas to diverse audiences.

- Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business Administration, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.


About Navi

We are on a mission to make finance simple, accessible and affordable for a billion Indians. Guided by a strong customer-first approach, we are building tech-first solutions that work at scale. Our offerings include a range of financial products and services across Loans, Insurance, Mutual Funds, Digital Gold and UPI.


Founded by Sachin Bansal & Ankit Agarwal in 2018, we are one of India’s fastest-growing financial services organisations. But we’re just getting started!


Our Culture

At Navi, we’re a place where ambition meets opportunity and ideas turn into impact quickly. We empower people with high ownership from the start, encouraging them to solve meaningful problems and build with excellence. Teams here work in an environment that values speed, collaboration and craftsmanship, while celebrating learning, growth and shared wins along the way. Whether you’re shaping the future of fintech products or driving innovation behind the scenes, life at Navi means being part of a high-energy, talent-driven workplace where your contributions truly make a difference.We’re guided by our own operating system - The Navi OS - a set of principles that shape how we work and win together. You can explore them at navi.com/our-values to see what drives us every day.


If this feels like you, Navi is the place to grow, thrive and make a real impact.
